danger_signals.visual_form: Temperature gauge in HUD; bike visual feedback during turbo abuse
danger_signals.meaning: Turbo boost overuse causes immobile cooldown period — player must manage thermal resource
Collision with rough surfaces causes speed loss or crash — environmental hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Breaks in the track; bottlenecks reducing lane count from 4 to 2
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial hazards requiring precise jump timing and lane positioning to avoid failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Bike orientation mid-air relative to landing surface
danger_signals.meaning: Poor landing geometry results in speed loss or crash — rewards skill in balance management

safety_signals.visual_form: Arrow graphics placed along the course
safety_signals.meaning: Driving over arrows resets engine temperature — safe points for thermal management

safety_signals.visual_form: Flat track terrain and aligned wheels on landing
safety_signals.meaning: Successful balanced landing maintains momentum — skill reward

safety_signals.visual_form: Four-lane track sections vs. two-lane bottlenecks
safety_signals.meaning: Wider track sections offer more routing flexibility and evasion options

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Computer-controlled opponent bikes rendered in distinct visual style from player bike
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player occupies foreground/center lane; opponents distributed across track lanes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct opponent bike designs; visually differentiated from player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupy competing lanes; ahead/behind player depending on race progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Course layout displayed; opponent position indicators; finish line marker
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Computer racers actively compete; player must achieve third place or higher to advance to championship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enhanced track difficulty; additional obstacles; bottleneck sections reduce lane count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Computer opponents more aggressive; greater environmental hazards; higher skill requirement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid-based editor interface; 19 types of hurdles selectable and placeable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No racing occurs; gameplay shifts to creative/construction mode; save/load functionality (Famicom only)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temperature gauge visual fills; bike rendering may show heat effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bike becomes immobilized until cooldown completes; player movement halted until thermal recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual scroll speed increases; bike rendering shows forward lean/acceleration pose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turbo boost active; enhanced velocity; increased difficulty managing landing angles and obstacle avoidance

environmental_storytelling.example: Early races feature simple layouts; qualification requires third-place finish; championship races add gaps, rough patches, and bottlenecks progressively
Four-lane sections offer freedom; two-lane bottlenecks force tactical positioning — visual communication of pressure/constraint states
Temperature reset arrows distributed along course indicate safe recovery points; their positioning suggests intended thermal management rhythm
Each opponent bike rendered distinctly; visual variety communicates multiple rival personalities without explicit character development

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: A button input increases speed; B button activates turbo (with thermal cost)
D-pad controls mid-air balance; landing alignment directly affects post-jump speed and stability
Immobilized cooldown period; forward movement halted until temperature resets via arrow collision or time passage
Move along track lanes; maintain position ahead/behind player; responsive to course hazards; accelerate and decelerate based on track conditions
Upon contact: speed reduction or crash; visual feedback communicates hazard severity through momentum loss
Requires precise timing and lane selection; failed execution results in crash; successful navigation maintains momentum

notes: Excitebike is a mechanically-driven communication system. Visual world-state is primarily conveyed through track layout, obstacle placement, and HUD temperature feedback rather than narrative or faction signaling. The game has minimal storytelling infrastructure; instead, manifestation patterns operate through spatial design and physics feedback. The player's bike and opponent bikes serve as primary visual faction markers—differentiation through rendering style communicates competitive relationship. In Design Mode, the game shifts paradigm entirely: the track-building mechanic becomes the primary world-state communicator, with 19 hurdle types functioning as a visual vocabulary for course difficulty and theme. The game lacks explicit political, narrative, or faction-based world-state systems; all manifestations are mechanical/environmental.
